channel.title: Blog on Chuang YANG | Researcher of Robotics & Neuroscience
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on Chuang YANG | Researcher of Robotics & Neuroscience
channel.copyright: 版权所有 © 2020 杨闯 All Rights Reserved

item.title: Brain-inspired vector navigation
item.description: Brain-inspired vector navigation method for UAV based on model of multi-scale grid cells Abstract: Animals have excellent ability to perform vector navigation, which provides a useful biological model for developing a very promising paradigm of autonomous intelligent goal-directed navigation for UAV working in unknown environment.

item.title: Navigation Cell-like Patterns Trained by Artificial Neural Networks
item.description: Emergence of Grid-like Representations by Training Recurrent Neural Networks to Perform Spatial Localization Abstract: Decades of research on the neural code underlying spatial navigation have re- vealed a diverse set of neural response properties.

item.title: The Brain Maps Out Diverse Behavioral Tasks Like Spaces
item.description: The Brain Maps Out Ideas and Memories Like Spaces Abstract: In the parable of the blind men and the elephant (as shown in the header image of the page), each paid attention to a different aspect of the creature.
